name: proof-wall-acceptance
description: Testable acceptance contract for the Hedera Proof Wall demo template. The validator (evaluator) agent grades the running app against these numbered assertions by driving it in a browser. This contract — not the raw PRD — is the source of truth for the semantic pass/fail gate.

evaluationRules.failOnUncertainty: If an assertion cannot be positively verified from the running app, mark it FAILED, not passed. Absence of evidence is a failure.
evaluationRules.credentials: The app must load, render, and be browsable without any .env file, private keys, operator keys, or funded testnet account. Never treat missing credentials as a passing excuse for a broken read path.
evaluationRules.walletGatedFlows: When the harness provides a Test Signer in the validator prompt: assertions flagged executableWithTestSigner=true MUST be executed end-to-end (connect burner wallet, complete the flow, verify on-chain via mirror node). Other walletRequired assertions without that flag remain affordance-only — verify the control is present and communicates the wallet/testnet requirement; do not require a completed tx. When no Test Signer is provided, treat all walletRequired assertions as affordance-only. A crash, blank screen, or unhandled error on the wallet path is always a failure.
evaluationRules.onChainVerification: For executableWithTestSigner assertions, UI success toasts alone are insufficient. Confirm effects via the Hedera testnet mirror node REST API (https://testnet.mirrornode.hedera.com): topics, topic messages, tokens, and/or contract results as relevant. Poll up to ~30s for mirror lag. Cite mirror evidence in the verdict.
evaluationRules.evidence: Every verdict issue must cite what was observed (route visited, element found/absent, console output, and mirror node responses for executed on-chain flows).

severityMeaning.critical: Route/app is broken or a core journey is impossible; run cannot pass.
severityMeaning.major: A required PRD capability is missing or non-functional on the read/affordance level.
severityMeaning.minor: Coherence, clarity, or documentation gap that degrades the demo but does not break a journey.

assertions.statement: The home route renders a Proof Wall without connecting a wallet and without any runtime/console errors.
assertions.howToVerify: Navigate to /. Confirm the page loads, primary content is visible, and the browser console shows no uncaught errors or forbidden error strings (Internal Server Error, Application error, Unhandled Runtime Error).

assertions.statement: The home page identifies which Hedera network and which HCS topic the wall is reading from (testnet by default).
assertions.howToVerify: On /, look for visible indication of the network (e.g. Testnet) and the active topic identifier or an explicit 'no topic configured' state.

assertions.statement: The home page presents a feed of proof/messages with consensus timestamps (or a clear empty state when the topic has no messages).
assertions.howToVerify: On /, locate a feed/list region for proofs. Each entry should convey its payload and a consensus timestamp; an explicit empty-state message is acceptable when no messages exist. A perpetual spinner or silent blank area is a failure.

assertions.statement: The read path (browsing proofs) is fully usable without a connected wallet.
assertions.howToVerify: Without connecting any wallet, confirm the proof feed and network/topic information are reachable and not blocked behind a wallet-connect wall.

assertions.statement: The app exposes a clear entry point to submit a new proof to the active HCS topic.
assertions.howToVerify: From /, find a control to submit a proof (a form or a button leading to one) that accepts proof content (text/JSON payload). The control must be present and reachable even if submission itself is wallet-gated.

assertions.statement: The submit-proof flow gracefully handles the no-wallet state, prompting connection or explaining the requirement instead of crashing.
assertions.howToVerify: Interact with the submit control without a wallet connected. Expect a connect prompt or clear guidance/feedback. An unhandled error, crash, or dead control is a failure.

assertions.statement: The /admin route renders without runtime/console errors.
assertions.howToVerify: Navigate to /admin. Confirm the page loads and the console shows no uncaught errors or forbidden error strings.

assertions.statement: The admin page exposes a control to create a new HCS topic for the Proof Wall.
assertions.howToVerify: On /admin, locate a labeled control (button/form/section) for creating an HCS topic.

assertions.statement: The admin page exposes a control to create an HTS badge/token used to reward participants.
assertions.howToVerify: On /admin, locate a labeled control for creating an HTS badge/token.

assertions.statement: The admin page provides a place to surface the created identifiers (topic ID and token ID) back to the user.
assertions.howToVerify: On /admin, confirm there is a region that displays created IDs or their current/unset state, so an organizer could read back the topic ID and token ID after creation.

assertions.statement: The admin page communicates that live setup is wallet-signed and needs a funded testnet account.
assertions.howToVerify: On /admin, look for copy indicating a Hedera wallet connection is required and that live setup needs a funded testnet account.

assertions.statement: The /my-proofs route renders without runtime/console errors.
assertions.howToVerify: Navigate to /my-proofs. Confirm the page loads and the console shows no uncaught errors or forbidden error strings.

assertions.statement: The my-proofs page prompts the user to connect their wallet to view wallet-specific state.
assertions.howToVerify: On /my-proofs without a wallet, expect a clear connect-wallet prompt rather than an error or empty screen.

assertions.statement: The my-proofs page describes/holds space for the connected account's proofs and their badge/token balance or association state.
assertions.howToVerify: On /my-proofs, confirm the UI conveys where the user's own proofs and badge/token balance (or association state) will appear once connected.

assertions.statement: Badge eligibility is presented as distinct from merely having posted a proof.
assertions.howToVerify: On /my-proofs, confirm the badge/token state is framed separately from the list of submitted proofs (holding the badge is not implied purely by posting).

assertions.statement: A wallet-connect affordance is available in the app shell / navigation across routes.
assertions.howToVerify: On any route, confirm a consistent connect-wallet affordance is present in the header/nav or page shell.

assertions.statement: The full app boots and all three routes are reachable with no .env, private keys, or funded account present.
assertions.howToVerify: With no credentials configured, visit /, /admin, and /my-proofs. All must render their intended UI (read paths live, wallet paths gated but graceful). Any route that hard-fails due to missing credentials is a failure.

assertions.statement: The three routes tell one coherent HCS + HTS story: HCS proofs on the wall and in my-proofs, HTS badge/token created in admin and reflected in my-proofs.
assertions.howToVerify: Browse all three routes and confirm the consensus-service (proof messages) and token-service (badge) concepts are consistently named and connected across the demo, not siloed or contradictory.

assertions.statement: The demo surfaces no Solidity/contract-deploy or local-chain tooling flows (no Hardhat/Foundry/Docker deploy UI or instructions).
assertions.howToVerify: Across the routes and any visible setup guidance in the UI, confirm there are no contract-deploy, Hardhat, Foundry, or local EVM deploy steps presented to the user.

assertions.statement: Connecting the burner wallet with the harness test signer key succeeds and the app shell shows a connected account.
assertions.howToVerify: Inject the test signer private key into localStorage (burnerWallet.pk), reload, open Connect Wallet, choose Burner Wallet from the Development group. Confirm the header/nav shows a connected account (EVM address or Hedera account ID). Retry briefly if account-id resolution lags on the mirror node.

assertions.statement: Creating an HCS topic with the test signer completes and surfaces the new topic ID; the topic exists on the testnet mirror node.
assertions.howToVerify: With the burner wallet connected, use the admin create-topic control. Confirm the UI shows a new topic ID (0.0.x). Verify via GET https://testnet.mirrornode.hedera.com/api/v1/topics/{topicId} (or that a create transaction for the test account appears). Poll up to ~30s for mirror lag.

assertions.statement: Submitting a proof with the test signer completes; the message appears in the topic's mirror node messages and on the Proof Wall UI.
assertions.howToVerify: With burner wallet connected and a topic configured (from C21 or an existing topic), submit a distinctive proof payload. Expect success feedback in the UI. Confirm GET https://testnet.mirrornode.hedera.com/api/v1/topics/{topicId}/messages contains the payload (payer should be the test account). Confirm the proof appears on the wall feed. Poll up to ~30s for mirror lag.
